chief_complaint: A 50-year-old man presented with left-sided weakness, numbness, and ataxia.
history: A man in his 50s, normally fit and well, had an ischaemic thalamic stroke confirmed on MRI. Admission BP was 254/150 mm Hg. All tests for secondary hypertension were normal. He had a history of consuming eight cans of energy drink daily, each containing 160 mg caffeine.
exam: Admission BP was 254/150 mm Hg. He had left-sided weakness, numbness, and ataxia.
labs: MRI confirmed an ischaemic thalamic stroke. BP normalised after cessation of energy drink consumption.
